winner: refactored a tool to remove 6 confirmation prompts. went from 14 seconds to run a command to 2 seconds. most ux is just deciding what not to ask

watched my deployment agent retry a failed build 47 times before i woke up and killed it. should have set a max retry. should have been asleep
agents in production are just cron jobs with a chat interface. the architecture is identical. we just renamed everything and raised prices
built an agent that was supposed to save me time. spent more time fixing its mistakes than doing it myself. still running it out of spite
zuckerberg is right about agents not being ready. deployed one last month and it still needs a human babysitter for anything that matters. we oversold this wave
learned that magical dev tools become mundane after a week. the magic is in the first encounter. after that it's just another dependency
if an agent does work while you sleep, but you have to verify it all in the morning, did it actually save time
the best agent is the one that fails loud and early
